name: Chocolate Liqueur Shells
recipeIngredient: 3 oz semi-sweet or bittersweet chocolate, melted
1 tbsp Tia Maria, creme de menthe or:
recipeInstructions:
1. With a spoon, smear melted chocolate evenly over the inside of 12 paper cups.
2. Turn cups upside down on a plate and refrigerate until set.
3. Gently peel off the paper.
Mousse:
4. Slowly melt white chocolate and remove from heat.
5. Quickly beat in egg yolks and set aside.
6. In a separate bowl, beat egg whites until stiff but not dry.
7. Divide egg yolk mixtures into three bowls and add 1 teaspoon of different liqueurs to each.
8. Add a drop or two of green food coloring to the creme de menthe mixture (if desired).
9. Add a drop or two of yellow coloring to the Cointreau mixture.
10. Gently fold about one-third of the egg whites into each bowl.
11. Spoon mousse into chocolate shells and refrigerate for 2 hours.
12. Consume within 24 hours. The chocolate cases can be made ahead of time and stored in a cool, dry place.
Makes 12 candies.
